_doc: The capability-TYPE registry — one row per capability TYPE, the orthogonal index that BOTH the provides/requires TAGS (Layer A, ops/cli/capabilities.py:CAPABILITY_TAGS) and the per-model capability FIELDS (Layer B, ops/model_capabilities.json {value,source}) reference. This is the net-new primitive of the capability-resolution architecture (CAPABILITY-RESOLUTION-DESIGN-CORRECTED.md §1 Layer C). registry-is-truth (AGENTS.md rule 8): this FILE is the single source; runtime/capabilities/resolver.py loads it at import (fail-loud if missing/malformed/empty). Adding a NEW capability = adding ONE entry HERE (no code edit). DELIBERATE 3-layer vocabulary, NOT a rename: the design KEEPS `thinking` and `reasoning` as distinct tags (a rename would cascade across every role's requires + resolve_binding + the projection) — do NOT collapse them. Each row's contract: id · value_shape (bool | enum | int | profile-ref — DRIVES override-injection: a `bool` capability_override injects-when-truthy, an `enum` selects its serve fragment by value via `serve_values`) · layer (which of A/B it surfaces as) · order (the canonical LAUNCH-ORDER rank — serve_flags injects an override-supplied capability into the ordered list at this rank; the family's own `capabilities` list is already in rank order. registry-is-truth: the order lives HERE, not as a code constant) · declare (where stated) · serve_ref (the key the stack-expression registry looks up to emit launch flags) · serve_values (enum-only: maps each enum value to its serve_ref, or null = no-op/no flag — e.g. vision.full emits nothing) · use_ref (how the runtime requests it + the gate, or null = serve-only) · verify (an optional probe — VERIFICATION, never the source of the declaration) · on_unexpressible (a stack that can't express it FAILs LOUD — a surfaced Notice + a recorded Gap, never a silent drop). The serve_ref values key rows in stack_capabilities.json.

quantization.declare: family attribute (the vLLM --quantization method) — e.g. the nemotron family's compressed-tensors

cpu_offload.declare: family attribute (the vLLM --cpu-offload-gb count) — offload N GiB of weights to CPU RAM (nemotron-30B needs 6 to fit Ada)

enforce_eager.declare: family attribute (the vLLM --enforce-eager switch — disable CUDA-graph capture; nemotron requires it)

pooling.declare: family default for the embed/pooling family (the vLLM --runner pooling switch)

embeddings.declare: family default; surfaces as the `embed` provides-tag (a pooling-runner model)
embeddings.use_ref: send to the /v1/embeddings endpoint (serve-only — no per-request capability field)

rerank.declare: family default; surfaces as the `rerank` provides-tag (a cross-encoder/late-interaction scorer)

tools.declare: family default + per-model override; surfaces as the `tools` provides-tag (Layer A) AND the `tools` field (Layer B)
tools.use_ref: send body.tools + tool_choice when the role wants a tool call (LIVE-phase gate)
tools.verify: auto tool_choice probe — a probe-false WARNS + marks source='unverified'; it NEVER overrides the declaration (verification-not-source)

reasoning.declare: family default + per-model override; surfaces as the `thinking`/`reasoning` provides-tags (Layer A) AND the `thinking` field (Layer B)
reasoning.use_ref: per-request enable_thinking (LIVE-phase wiring; serve-only in the SAFE half)
reasoning._note: the design keeps BOTH `thinking` and `reasoning` tags; this TYPE row indexes the family-level reasoning intent, not a rename of either tag.

structured_output.declare: the `json_schema` capability FIELD (Layer B). DELIBERATELY a field, NOT a provides-tag (capabilities.py:78-79: adding it to `provides` would diverge from the seam)
structured_output.use_ref: send body.response_format={type:json_schema,...} iff capable AND the role has a schema; on 400 fail-loud fallback (LIVE-phase)

vision.declare: family default + per-model override; surfaces as the `vision` provides-tag. enum values: lm-only | full
vision._note: ENUM-selected serve fragment (the dissolution of BUG 2): `lm-only` selects the stack's `vision` serve_ref (→ --language-model-only, the text RHM skips the vision tower for VRAM); `full` maps to null = NO flag (the vision tower is loaded — the absence of --language-model-only IS full vision). An unknown enum value FAILS LOUD in the resolver. So a model declares `capability_overrides: {vision: 'lm-only'}` (chat-4b-fp8) and the resolver injects --language-model-only at order rank 40 (between reasoning 30 and trust_remote_code 90); a `full` model adds the tag but no flag.

kv_cache_dtype.declare: per-model override (a serve-home field) — surfaces as a Layer-B field when a model needs it

trust_remote_code.declare: family attribute (the vLLM --trust-remote-code switch) — emitted LAST in the canonical serve order to match the hand-authored flag sequence

context_window.declare: model runtime params (services.json config.max_model_len), NOT the family — surfaces as the `context_ceiling` field (Layer B)

sampling_profile.declare: family default (a named sampling profile, e.g. qwen3.5: thinking-on 1.0/0.95/20/1.5 vs non-thinking 0.7/0.8/20/1.5). No serve flag (a per-request use-knob) — no `order`.
